summary.intent: Fix a silent no-op bug in the dcg-guard hook where `dcg` binary was not resolvable on the gateway process PATH under systemd, causing destructive commands to pass through unchallenged, and then address subsequent PR review comments.
summary.outcome: Two commits were pushed to the `warm-pond` branch (PR #635): the initial fix adding PATH augmentation and loud warnings, followed by a second commit addressing reviewer feedback to refresh stale on-disk handlers via fingerprinting and switch to async `tokio::process::Command` for the version probe. All 5 tests pass and clippy is clean.

repo: Shell hooks always run in the gateway process environment, not the interactive shell — confirmed at `crates/plugins/src/shell_hook.rs:101-117`.
Systemd ExecStart with `bash -lc` still strips `$HOME/.local/bin` from the inherited environment, so tools installed via `uv tool install` or `pipx` are invisible to hook handlers.
The dcg-guard seeding logic was gated on `HOOK.md` existence, which meant existing installations kept stale pre-fix handlers while the startup log falsely reported the guard as active.
The `seed_*` sibling helpers in `prepare_gateway_core` use synchronous file I/O — this is an established convention in the codebase.

dcg cannot be installed via `cargo install` (the seeded HOOK.md previously gave this incorrect instruction); correct installers are `uv tool install destructive-command-guard` or `pipx install destructive-command-guard`.

code.finding: seed_dcg_guard_hook and log_dcg_guard_status were made async to allow use of tokio::process::Command for the dcg --version probe, avoiding blocking inside the async prepare_gateway_core.
Stale handler detection uses string fingerprinting: checks for 'export PATH=' in handler.sh and 'uv tool install destructive-command-guard' in HOOK.md to determine if files need refreshing.
Hooks execute in the gateway process environment — the ShellHookHandler was intentionally left untouched to avoid globally augmenting PATH for all hooks.

workflow: macOS local `just lint` times out on unrelated CUDA feature builds; the workaround is to lint the specific gateway crate directly with `--features local-llm-metal`.
Greptile automated review caught both the stale-handler false-positive (P1) and the blocking subprocess in async context (P2) before human reviewers, providing actionable structured feedback.

Fingerprint-based file refresh (checking for marker strings rather than version numbers) is a pragmatic approach for auto-remediating deployed config files without requiring version tracking infrastructure.

friction: macOS `just lint` times out on unrelated CUDA feature builds, requiring direct per-crate clippy invocation as a workaround.

The original seeding logic's `if !hook_md.exists()` guard created a silent false-positive: existing broken installations appeared healthy at startup, making the bug invisible to operators post-deployment.
open_items: `just lint` on Linux CI has not yet run — macOS local lint was done as a workaround.
`just test` full suite on CI is pending.
Manual QA on a real systemd deployment needed: delete hook dir, restart Moltis, confirm exactly one startup log line appears.
Manual QA with dcg installed: verify `rm -rf` through exec tool is blocked.
Manual QA with dcg uninstalled: verify `NOT scanned` warning appears in gateway logs per invocation.
